data.title: Mercantile Bank declares $0.39 dividend
data.description:
• Mercantile Bank (MBWM) declares $0.39/share quarterly dividend, in line with previous.
  • Forward yield 2.93%
  • Payable June 17; for shareholders of record June 5; ex-div June 5.
  • See MBWM Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Earnings Snapshot: Danaher tops Q1 earnings targets, lifts FY26 guidance despite slight revenue miss
France sees €4B-€6B impact from Iran crisis amid rising yields, finance minister says
data.description:
France Finance Minister Roland Lescure said that France has incurred an economic cost of €4B to €6B due to the ongoing Iran crisis driven largely by surging energy prices and rising borrowing costs.

Bond yields have surged since the onset of the Middle East conflict, with the resulting rise in government borrowing costs alone adding roughly €3.6B to the budget, Lescure told France's RTL radio.

Prime Minister Sebastien Lecornu's administration pledged to fully offset the fiscal impact of measures introduced to shield households from the energy price shock. Lescure added that he will present plans to freeze certain areas of public spending during a meeting with lawmakers on Tuesday.

France, which already runs one of the largest budget deficits in the eurozone, can afford support measures that are strictly targeted at those most in need. Far-right parties have called for a reduction in the 20% value-added tax on fuel, while left-wing groups are pushing for broader interventions, including caps on energy prices.

The Iran war has pushed up inflation due to a surge in energy prices and strained energy-intensive sectors like transport, agriculture, and fishing.

data.title: Synchrony Financial declares $0.30 dividend, $0.34 from next quarter, $6.5B buy back program
data.description:
• Synchrony Financial (SYF) declares $0.30/share quarterly dividend, in line with previous.
  • Forward yield 1.53%
  • Payable May 15; for shareholders of record May 5; ex-div May 5.
  • Additionally, the Company plans to increase the quarterly cash dividend from $0.30 to $0.34 per share of common stock beginning in third quarter 2026.
  • The Company also approved a new common share repurchase program of up to $6.5 billion, replacing prior program, which was scheduled to expire on June 30, 2026 and had approximately $300 million remaining.
  • The program commences in the second quarter of 2026 and does not have an expiration date. 
  • The new share repurchase program replaces the Company's  
  • See SYF Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Peoples Bancorp GAAP EPS of $0.81 beats by $0.01, revenue of $119.3M beats by $0.58M
data.description:
• Peoples Bancorp press release (PEBO): Q1 GAAP EPS of $0.81 beats by $0.01.
  • Revenue of $119.3M (+5.6% Y/Y) beats by $0.58M.
  • Net interest margin for the first quarter of 2026 increased to 4.16% when compared to 4.12% for the linked quarter driven by a reduction in deposit costs.
  • The provision for credit losses was $9.7 million for the first quarter of 2026, compared to $8.1 million for the linked quarter and $10.2 million for the first quarter of 2025.

data.title: Synchrony Financial GAAP EPS of $2.27 beats by $0.07, revenue of $4.77B beats by $1B
data.description:
• Synchrony Financial press release (SYF): Q1 GAAP EPS of $2.27 beats by $0.07.
  • Revenue of $4.77B (+3.5% Y/Y) beats by $1B.
  • Purchase volume increased 6% to $43.0 billion.
  • Loan receivables were flat at $100.1 billion.
  • Average active accounts decreased 1% to 68.8 million.
  • Net interest margin increased 76 basis points to 15.50%.
  • Efficiency ratio increased 220 basis points to 35.6%.
  • Return on assets increased 20 basis points to 2.7%.
  • Return on equity increased 110 basis points to 19.5%.
  • Return on tangible common equity** increased 210 basis points to 24.5%.
  • Book value per share increased 12% to $45.29.
  • Tangible book value per share** increased 8% to $37.62
  • The company announced that the Board of Directors approved a new share repurchase program of up to $6.5 billion of the Company’s common stock, which commences in second quarter 2026 and, in a change from our prior share repurchase programs, does not have an expiration date.
  • The new share repurchase program replaces the Company’s prior program, which was scheduled to expire on June 30, 2026.
  • In addition, the Board approved a planned 13% increase in the quarterly cash dividend to $0.34 per share of common stock beginning in third quarter 2026.

data.title: UnitedHealth to buy back at least $2B in stock by Q2 2026
data.description:
• UnitedHealth Group (UNH) Tuesday said it has agreed to repurchase at least $2B of its common stock by the end of the second quarter of 2026.
  • UnitedHealth Group said it completed the sale of the Optum UK business, with $400M in net proceeds committed to the United Health Foundation.

data.title: Danaher Non-GAAP EPS of $2.06 beats by $0.12, revenue of $5.95B misses by $50M
data.description:
• Danaher press release (DHR): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $2.06 beats by $0.12.
  • Revenue of $5.95B (+3.7% Y/Y) misses by $50M.

  • For the second quarter 2026, the Company anticipates that non-GAAP core revenue will increase in the low-single digit percent range year-over-year.

    For full year 2026, the Company continues to expect that non-GAAP core revenue will increase in the 3% to 6% range year-over-year vs 4.26% consensus.  The Company is also increasing its full year adjusted diluted net earnings per common share guidance to a range of $8.35 to $8.55 ($8.40 consensus)  versus previous guidance of $8.35 to $8.50.

data.title: UnitedHealth Non-GAAP EPS of $7.23 beats by $0.63, revenue of $111.72B beats by $2.06B
data.description:
• UnitedHealth press release (UNH): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $7.23 beats by $0.63.
  • Revenue of $111.72B (+2.0% Y/Y) beats by $2.06B.
  • Full Year 2026 Earnings Outlook Raised to Greater Than $17.35 Per Share; Adjusted Earnings of Greater Than $18.25 Per Share vs $17.87 consensus
  • UnitedHealth Group’s medical cost ratio was 83.9% for the first quarter 2026, down 90 basis points from the first quarter 2025. The operating cost ratio of 13.8% in the first quarter 2026 compared to 12.4% in the first quarter 2025, reflecting investments to drive improved consumer and care provider experiences and greater operating efficiencies, as well as investments in its people and the communities it serves.
  • The operating cost ratio of 13.8% reflected incremental investments in people, processes and technology, including artificial intelligence, to improve performance and support future innovations, modernization, growth and earnings.
  • UnitedHealthcare served 49.1 million consumers and expanded operating margins by 40 basis points to 6.6% compared to 6.2% in the first quarter 2025.
  • Optum supported more than 122 million consumers across its businesses, driving revenues of $63.7 billion and earnings of $3.3 billion, reflecting a margin of 5.2%.
  • Optum Health operating earnings were $1.1 billion. Adjusted operating earnings were $1.3 billion, excluding the impacts related to the third-party loss contracts and restructuring actions disclosed in the fourth quarter 2025.
  •  
  • Shares +2.7% PM.

data.title: AM Markets Need to Know: Apple's new CEO, Musk boosts SpaceX stake, and more
data.description:
Stock index futures were lower on Monday as retail investors began moving capital back into equities following a period of war-related caution.

Now, here are 5 news stories that broke overnight to watch out for:

Apple names new CEO: Apple (AAPL) has named John Ternus as its next chief executive, succeeding Tim Cook. Ternus is a longtime Apple executive who joined the company in 2001 and currently serves as senior vice president of hardware engineering, overseeing development of key products including the iPhone, iPad, Mac, Apple Watch, and AirPods.

Retail investors poised to drive rally: Retail investors are set to fuel the next leg of the stock market rally as they begin moving capital back into equities, according to Tom Lee, Fundstrat’s head of research. In an interview with CNBC, Lee explained that while these investors initially pulled back during the buildup to the U.S.-Israel conflict with Iran, they are now “beginning to take money off the sidelines and buy stocks.”

Musk boosts SpaceX stake: Elon Musk increased his stake in SpaceX (SPACE) last year after buying $1.4B worth of shares from current and former employees, The Information reported, citing a draft of SpaceX’s confidential IPO prospectus. The document also outlined a plan to award Musk tens of millions of additional shares if SpaceX’s market capitalization rises by trillions of dollars.

Michael Burry on market outlook: Michael Burry said the record stock rally is unlikely to end in a sudden crash, calling a “needle top” unprecedented in history. The investor of “The Big Short” fame predicted choppy trading going forward, adding that this run-up in stocks could form part of the bull market’s peak when investors look back in the future.

Golden Pass ships first LNG cargo: A tanker has arrived at the Golden Pass facility in Texas to collect the plant’s inaugural export of liquefied natural gas, Golden Pass LNG said Monday, after finally beginning production earlier this year following prolonged construction delays. The Al Qaiyyah LNG tanker is owned by QatarEnergy, which holds a 70% stake in the plant; Exxon Mobil (XOM) holds the remaining 30%.
